steps.description: Dissolve CsI in deionized water.
Dissolve BiI3 in methanol.

Mix the CsI aqueous solution with the BiI3 methanol solution and vigorously stir with magnetic bars for 1 h at room temperature. A color change and powder precipitation are immediately visible upon mixing.

equipment.name: magnetic stirrer
equipment.settings: vigorous stirring for 1 h at room temperature

steps.description: Centrifuge the solution at 2000 r/min for 5 min, then pour off the supernatant.
Dry the remaining precipitates in a centrifuge tube at 80 °C in a vacuum oven overnight.

synthesis.notes: The synthesis follows the reaction: 3CsI + 2BiI3 → Cs3Bi2I9. A color change and powder precipitation are immediately visible upon mixing the precursor solutions. The resulting powders can subsequently be consolidated into pellets by spark plasma sintering (SPS) at peak temperatures of 200–300 °C.

steps.description: Stoichiometric amounts of calcium hydroxide were first dispersed into deionized water.
Phosphoric acid was poured into the calcium hydroxide suspension.
Aggressive agitation was carried out on a magnetic stir-plate at 300 r/min for 10 min.
The HA powders were precipitated out in the beaker.
The precipitated HA powders were dried in a digitally-controlled oven.

steps.description: Dissolve CsI in deionized water and BiI3 in methanol separately.

Mix CsI solution in deionized water with BiI3 solution in methanol and vigorously stir by magnetic bars for 1 h at room temperature. A color change and powder precipitation are immediately visible upon mixing.
Centrifuge the solution at 2000 r/min for 5 min, pour off the supernatant.
Dry the remaining precipitates (Cs3Bi2I9) in a centrifuge tube at 80 °C in a vacuum oven overnight.

Synthesize hydroxyapatite (HA) powders by wet chemical deposition: disperse stoichiometric amounts of calcium hydroxide into deionized water, then pour phosphoric acid into the suspension and agitate at 300 r/min for 10 min. HA powders precipitate out.
Dry the precipitated HA powders in a digitally-controlled oven.
Mix equal masses of Cs3Bi2I9 with HA in a mortar and pestle.

Load ~1 g of mixed Cs3Bi2I9 + HA powders into a tungsten carbide die (inner diameter 6 mm) wrapped with graphite foil (0.20 mm thick). Consolidate by SPS at a peak temperature of 500 °C and maximum pressure of 400 MPa. Temperature increased from 25 to 500 °C at 100 °C/min; uniaxial pressure increased from 10 to 400 MPa at a steady rate to match temperature increase. Peak temperature and pressure held for 5 min, followed by air cool in Ar atmosphere. Current pattern: 25 ms on and 3 ms off.

equipment.name: Spark Plasma Sintering (SPS) system
equipment.settings: Model 10-3; 100 °C/min heating rate; 25 ms on / 3 ms off current pattern; peak 500 °C, 400 MPa, held 5 min

equipment.name: K-type thermocouple
equipment.settings: inserted into bottom punch ~2 mm below sample

steps.description: Air cool the sintered pellet in Ar atmosphere after holding at peak temperature and pressure.
Mechanically polish the sintered pellets in anhydrous ethanol with 1200 grit sandpaper, then store in a vacuum desiccator before characterization.

synthesis.notes: The composite consists of 50 wt% Cs3Bi2I9 and 50 wt% hydroxyapatite (HA). The Cs3Bi2I9 phase was first synthesized via solution-based precipitation (3CsI + 2BiI3 → Cs3Bi2I9), and HA was synthesized via wet chemical deposition (10Ca(OH)2 + 6H3PO4 → Ca10(PO4)6(OH)2 + 18H2O). The composite pellets achieved >90% theoretical density. The theoretical density of composite pellets was determined by the rule of mixture. Density was measured by Archimedes' method using anhydrous ethanol as suspension agent. The onset decomposition temperature for the composite is ~525 °C.
